questions.question: Vaigai River, which was seen in news, lies in which state?
questions.explanation: Madras High Court directed local bodies to submit an action plan to prevent pollution in the Vaigai River. Vaigai River originates from Varusanadu and Megamalai hills of the Western Ghats in Tamil Nadu. It flows southeast through the Pandya Nadu region, rarely flooding, and drains into the Palk Strait near Pamban Bridge. The river is 258 km long, with a basin area of 7,741 sq. km entirely in Tamil Nadu. Vaigai is fed by the Periyar Dam in Kerala, with water diverted through a tunnel in the Western Ghats. Vaigai is mentioned in Sangam literature and is central to Madurai, the ancient Pandya kingdom’s capital.

questions.question: Which two ministries organized the National Tribal Health Conclave 2025?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Rural Development and Ministry of Finance

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of Tribal Affairs and Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Ministry of Panchayati Raj

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Tribal Affairs and Ministry of Youth Affairs

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of Tribal Affairs and Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

questions.explanation: The National Tribal Health Conclave 2025 took place on January 20, 2025, at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi. It was organized by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs (MoTA) and the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoH&FW). The event is part of the Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan, aiming to address health and well-being challenges of India’s tribal communities. It focuses on improving healthcare access and outcomes for tribal populations facing unique health challenges.

questions.question: Parakram Diwas is celebrated on January 23 to commemorate the birth anniversary of which freedom fighter?
questions.explanation: Parakram Diwas 2025 is celebrated from January 23-25 at Barabati Fort in Cuttack, the birthplace of Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose. The event commemorates Bose’s 128th birth anniversary and honors his contributions to India’s freedom struggle. Parakram Diwas, observed annually on January 23, aims to instill fearlessness and patriotism, particularly among the youth. The celebration seeks to inspire resilience and encourage standing strong against challenges in life.

questions.question: Typhoon Missile System was developed by which country?
questions.explanation: The U.S. military has relocated Typhon missile launchers within the Philippines to improve mobility and survivability amid tensions with China and Russia. Typhon, or the Mid-Range Capability (MRC), is a mobile, surface-to-surface missile system developed by Lockheed Martin, United States. It features a modular design and can fire multiple missile types, including the SM-6 (500 km range) and Tomahawk cruise missiles (2,500 km range). The SM-6 strikes air and surface targets, while the Tomahawk is effective for land-attack and anti-ship missions. A full Typhon system includes four launchers, a command post, and support vehicles on trailers.

questions.question: Which country has organized the International Pashmina Festival in January 2025?
questions.explanation: Nepal organized its first three-day International Pashmina Festival to highlight Chyangra Pashmina, sourced from Pashmina goats. Nepal is a major producer of authentic Pashmina, known for its high quality. The wool is hand-combed, hand-spun, and woven using traditional methods with natural dyes and embroidery. The exhibition featured 150 stalls, including 40 showcasing pure Nepali Pashmina. Nepali Pashmina competes internationally, with strong markets in the US, Japan, and Europe. Challenges include raw material supply and the need for policy improvements to expand the market. Fake and blended Pashmina pose a challenge globally, especially in India.

questions.question: Etikoppaka toys, which were showcased at Republic Day 2025, belongs to which state?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh’s Etikoppaka toys were prominently featured at the Republic Day parade. They are traditional wooden toys made by artisans from Etikoppaka village, Andhra Pradesh, near the Varaha River. Natural dyes from seeds, bark, roots, leaves, and lacquer are used to color these toys. The softwood from ‘ankudu’ trees (Wrightia Tinctoria) is primarily used. The toys are rounded with no sharp edges, ensuring safety. Etikoppaka toys received the Geographical Indication (GI) tag in 2017.

questions.question: Which state government has launched the Chief Minister Mobile Operation Theatre (CM-MOT) to provide medical services to remote areas?
questions.explanation: Nagaland launched the Chief Minister’s Mobile Operation Theatre (CMMOT) on the 76th Republic Day. Governor La Ganesan and Chief Minister Neiphiu Rio flagged off the initiative at the secretariat plaza. CMMOT aims to provide advanced surgical and healthcare services to rural and remote areas. It is equipped with keyhole surgery capabilities, ultrasound, X-ray, endoscopy, and blood testing tools. A medical team from Kohimas Hospital, along with private and government doctors, will deliver healthcare services. The initiative seeks to enhance health equity and bridge gaps between urban and remote healthcare access.

questions.question: Recently, where was the Sci-Fi Science Film Festival organized in India?
questions.explanation: Goa Chief Minister Pramod Sawant inaugurated the 10th Sci-FI Science Film Festival of India in Panaji on 29 January 2025. The 4-day festival runs from 29 January to 1 February 2025 and is organized annually by the Goa government to promote scientific curiosity among students. The festival aims to inspire youth to pursue science and contribute to India’s development by 2047. It is organized by Vidnyan Parishad Goa with support from the Goa Science Council and Goa Science & Technology Department. The theme is Green Revolution, honoring Dr. M.S. Swaminathan, the father of India’s Green Revolution.

questions.question: Which state has become the first to start a rural cricket league?
questions.explanation: Bihar became the first state to launch a Rural Cricket League. Bihar Cricket Association (BCA) under President Rakesh Tiwari is organizing the Bihar Rural League (BRL). The league is for players who haven’t played at district or state levels. Players aged 13 to 23 from villages, schools, and colleges can participate. Talent hunts will be held in all districts, forming 16 teams per district. 570 district-level matches will be played in a knockout format. Top players will enter the Super League with 38 teams and 79 matches. A total of 649 matches will be played with 10,000 participants.

questions.question: Which city has become the fifth city in Maharashtra to have a climate-resilient roadmap?
questions.explanation: Mira Bhayander in Maharashtra launched its City Climate Action Plan 2024-2047, becoming the fifth city in the state with a climate-resilient roadmap. Four other cities are Mumbai, Sholapur, Nashik and Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar (formerly Aurangabad). The plan targets a 36% reduction in emissions by 2047 through renewable energy, energy-efficient buildings, and improved waste management. The city’s emissions in 2023 were 1.34 million tonnes CO2e, with 62% from stationary energy, 22% from transport, and 16% from waste. Six priority sectors include energy, water, urban flooding, greening, mobility, and waste management. Measures include flood early warning systems, rooftop solar adoption, green retrofitting, and decentralized wastewater treatment.
